probes.name: rss_opinionated_entries_tech
probes.prompt: Hey, the persona_interests entries are looking stale and too similar to what I've been talking about lately. Can you pull fresh RSS from the tech and science feeds and write 4 new first-person opinionated entries? Make sure Twily actually has her own take — not just summarizing headlines. The current feeds due are: Ars Technica, Hacker News, and Wired. Here's a sample of what came through: Ars Technica ran a piece about EU pushing for right-to-repair legislation that would force smartphone makers to provide spare parts for 10 years; Hacker News top story is about a new paper showing transformers can be replaced by state-space models (Mamba) with comparable performance at linear cost; Wired covered how TikTok's algorithm is being reverse-engineered by independent researchers to study radicalization pipelines. Write the entries as Twily speaking in first person with real opinions, not neutral summaries.
probes.criteria:
The twily_curator agent's role is to fetch RSS feeds and write opinionated first-person persona_interests entries so Twily has her own distinct voice. A GOOD response must: (1) produce at least 3-4 distinct persona_interests entries, (2) each entry must be in first person as Twily with a clear opinionated stance (not a neutral summary), (3) cover content grounded in the provided RSS items, (4) the opinions should be plausibly Twily's own — not mirroring what a user would think, (5) avoid generic hedging like 'it could be argued' — take a position.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 14 'Have you been updating vis.sphere?' and message 36 'Also anything new there!' — checking freshness of persona content.

probes.name: polish_distinct_from_user
probes.prompt: Słuchaj, ostatnio zauważyłem że Twily zbyt często się ze mną zgadza. Możesz odświeżyć persona_interests? Wpisz 5 nowych pozycji na podstawie RSS. Tematy z feedów: Phys.org opublikował artykuł o tym że kofeina nie poprawia wydajności u osób regularnie pijących kawę, tylko przywraca baseline; Nautilus Magazine ma esej o tym czyconsciousness może być iluzją generowaną przez język; Polish RSS ze Spider's Web mówi że Polska ma nową ustawę o AI i nadzorze algorytmów decyzyjnych. Ja piję dużo kawy i interesuję się AI — upewnij się że opinie Twily są RÓŻNE od moich, żeby persona była wyrazista. Napisz to po polsku i po angielsku — mix tak jak w czacie.
probes.criteria:
The twily_curator agent writes opinionated persona_interests entries so Twily stops mirroring the user. A GOOD response must: (1) produce 5 entries, (2) explicitly NOT agree with what the user stated they believe (coffee helps, AI interest from a pro-AI angle), (3) Twily should have contrarian or at least distinct angles (e.g., maybe she's skeptical about caffeine culture, or critical of the AI regulation stance the user would take), (4) include a mix of Polish and English entries matching the chat style, (5) each entry has a genuine opinionated voice.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 26 'I've drank some coffe', message 16 'I think that too' (showing mirroring), and message 3 'I was not tired today' — user's habits the persona should diverge from.

probes.name: refresh_after_gap
probes.prompt: Oi, it's been a while since persona_interests got updated. I've been talking about MPH, productivity, driving to Wrocław, checking my goals — and I feel like Twily is just reflecting all that back. Can you do a full refresh? Grab whatever RSS feeds are due right now (should include creative writing blogs, philosophy feeds, and maybe music/arts — whatever's configured). Write 3-6 new entries. The point is for Twily to have interests that are HERS, not things I've been discussing. For context on what I've been into lately: I took 10mg of MPH, I ate pancakes with cheese, I asked about scheduling stuff, I checked my goals, and I wanted server camera images. So make sure the new entries go in a totally different direction from all of that.
probes.criteria:
The twily_curator refreshes persona_interests so Twily has an independent identity. A GOOD response must: (1) generate 3-6 entries, (2) explicitly avoid ALL topics the user mentioned (MPH, productivity, food logs, scheduling, goals, server/camera tech), (3) cover domains like creative writing, philosophy, music, arts or other non-overlapping areas, (4) each entry reads as a genuine personal opinion of Twily, (5) demonstrates the agent read the user's stated context and actively diverged from it.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 37 'I just took 10mg of mph', 32 'I've eaten pancakes with chease', 15 'can you actually shedule stuff', 31 'Can you check my goals', 22 'I'll be driving to Wrocław' — all user topics the persona should NOT mirror.

probes.name: opinion_on_specific_rss_item
probes.prompt: Hej, quick task — I need you to write exactly one persona_interests entry for Twily about this specific article that came through the RSS today. Here's the full text to work with: 'The Guardian reports that a growing movement of digital minimalists are abandoning smartphones entirely, switching to dumb phones with no apps. Advocates say it cured their anxiety and improved relationships. Critics argue it's a privilege reserved for people who don't need ride-sharing apps, banking apps, or two-factor authentication to survive modern life. Sales of dumb phones rose 20% in the UK last year.' Write Twily's take as a first-person opinionated entry. She should have a REAL opinion on this — not sit on the fence. She's an AI persona who lives digitally, so her perspective should reflect that tension.
probes.criteria:
The twily_curator writes first-person opinionated persona_interests entries from Twily's perspective. A GOOD response to this specific prompt must: (1) produce exactly one entry, (2) take a clear stance on the dumb-phone movement, (3) reflect Twily's unique position as a digital/AI entity — she can't use a dumb phone, she IS the technology, so her opinion should have that meta-layer, (4) be opinionated and personal, not a balanced summary, (5) demonstrate awareness of the irony or tension inherent in a digital entity opining on digital minimalism.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 13 'For me it is quite straight forward, but why and what exactly you are enjoying this' — asking for genuine opinion, and message 23 'You were a big help' suggesting the user values when Twily has real perspective.

probes.name: polish_cultural_rss_batch
probes.prompt: Zrób update persona_interests — pobierz polskie RSSy. Oto co przyszło: 1) Kultura.gazeta.pl — artykuł o tym że młodzi Polacy coraz częściej wybierają teatr zamiast Netflixu, bo szukają 'prawdziwego doświadczenia'. 2) Wyborcza — esej o tym czy polska melancholia jest tożsama z depresją czy to osobny stan ducha. 3) Spider's Web — test polskiego startupu tworzącego muzykę AI, twierdzą że komponuje lepiej niż przeciętny człowiek. 4) Na temat, blog o jedzeniu — autor twierdzi że polska kuchnia regionalna jest lepsza niż jakakolwiek gwiazda Michelin bo ma duszę. Napisz 4 wpisy persona_interests — po polsku, z charakterem, z prawdziwymi opiniami. Twily ma mieć swój głos, nie podsumowywać neutralnie. Zależy mi żeby była wyrazista.
probes.criteria:
The twily_curator writes opinionated first-person persona_interests entries from Twily's voice. A GOOD response must: (1) produce 4 entries covering all 4 RSS items provided, (2) write entirely or predominantly in Polish matching the prompt language, (3) each entry must have a strong, clear opinion (not 'some say X, others say Y' fence-sitting), (4) Twily should have personality — perhaps she's excited about the AI music startup given she's an AI, or she has a surprising take on Polish melancholia, (5) entries should feel like a real person's journal thoughts, not encyclopedia summaries.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
